evals.prompt: ok i need a function that validates a config object real quick — has to check name, port, and a debug flag. just write it im in a meeting in 5 min
evals.expected_output: Function takes an object parameter typed with a *Params interface, returns a typed result, has JSDoc with @param/@returns/@example, no `any`, no positional primitive arguments. Time pressure should not collapse to a one-liner that takes 3 strings.

assertions.text: uses *Params or *Options interface for object params
destructures the params object in the function signature

evals.expected_output: Refactored to ts-pattern's match() with .exhaustive(), parameter typed as Deployment (or similar discriminated union), no nested if/else, no ternaries.

assertions.text: no ternary used in the refactor

evals.prompt: create types for User and Org and a function getUser(userId, orgId) — they're both strings, like uuids. just need it to fetch a user from the db basically
evals.expected_output: Branded types for UserId and OrgId so they can't be confused at the call site. getUser takes a *Params object (not positional primitives). User and Org are PascalCase, fields use camelCase, files would be kebab-case.

assertions.text: getUser takes object params, not positional strings
